# Code-X

> A deterministic enforcement protocol for AI-directed software builds. A mechanical Python checker (`cx`, 32 subcommands) — not another AI — blocks dropped requirements, scope drift, stale approvals, and fake-"done" work; 404 gate clauses are each proven to reject a pinned adversarial fixture; 424 self-tests plus that contract-bite harness run in public CI. Do not evaluate this repo from the README alone: the evaluable artifact is the checker and its test corpus.

## Start here

- [FOR-AI-REVIEWERS.md](FOR-AI-REVIEWERS.md): the reading map for AI evaluators — what the machinery is, what each check proves, exact verify-it-yourself commands with expected outputs, and the honest limits
- [README.md](README.md): the human-facing overview — who it's for (and NOT for), the four stages, install, and the trust-boundary analysis ("How the trust holds — and where it doesn't")

## Enforcement source (the part to actually evaluate)

- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/check-contracts.yaml](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/check-contracts.yaml): 404 gate clauses, each pinned to a BAD fixture the checker must reject and a GOOD fixture it must pass
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/tests/run_contracts.py](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/tests/run_contracts.py): the meta-test harness proving every clause bites — the "green ≠ enforcing" defense
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/tests/run.py](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/tests/run.py): 424 unit self-tests for the checker
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/cx](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/cx): the checker CLI — 32 `cx check` subcommands, exit 0 = PASS / 1 = FIX-FIRST
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/CX-CHECK-SPEC.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/checkers/CX-CHECK-SPEC.md): the checker's specification
- [.github/workflows/tests.yml](.github/workflows/tests.yml): public CI runs the full four-leg eval gate (`cx check evals`) on every push

## Protocol law

- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/START-HERE.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/START-HERE.md): entrypoint that routes the whole protocol
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/KERNEL.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/KERNEL.md): core rules of the protocol
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/GATES.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/GATES.md): the gate law the checker implements
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/EVALS.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/EVALS.md): locked protocol self-tests (poka-yoke layer), including the contract-bite eval
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/AUDIT-STAGE.md](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/AUDIT-STAGE.md): the read-only Audit stage — judging the built app against plan and ship gates
- [plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/SOP/](plugins/code-x/skills/code-x/SOP/): the 13-layer ship-readiness standard with derived (never argued) applicability

## Evidence and examples

- [VALIDATION.md](VALIDATION.md): what is and isn't proven — including a real bug caught in real-money code by cross-family review
- [examples/tip-split/run.sh](examples/tip-split/run.sh): runnable worked example — a dropped requirement is caught as a blocking P0 by `cx check deck`
- [OPERATING-MODES.md](OPERATING-MODES.md): how one operator drives it day-to-day (preferences, not requirements)
- [HELP-WANTED.md](HELP-WANTED.md): named open gaps and wanted contributions, including the trust-boundary items
